title: Filtering lables with regular expressions?
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The release notes for podman 4 say:

> All commands that support filtering their output based on labels (e.g. podman volume ls, podman ps) now support labels specified using regular expressions (e.g. `--filter label=some.prefix.com/key/*`).

But:

- The example shown only makes sense as a glob-style wildcard, not as a regular expression.
- The `podman ps` man page doesn't mention this feature.
- In practice, it appears that only glob-style wildcards work. Given a
  container with label `foo`:

  ```
  podman run -d --name=example --label foo=bar docker.io/alpine:latest sleep inf
  ```

  This (a glob style wildcard) works:

  ```
  $ podman ps --filter label='f*'
  CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                            COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS            PORTS       NAMES
  7321159b6520  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  sleep inf   6 minutes ago  Up 6 minutes ago              sleeper
  ```

  But this (a regular expression) does not:

  ```
  $ podman ps --filter label='f.*'
  ```

Is this a documentation error, or was this *supposed* to support
regular expressions?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Wed Jun 15 10:32:06 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Looks like an error on my part compiling the release notes - I interpreted https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/c050f05ccff6416ccd41d271f0051b395cbe5a1f as adding regular expression support when it actually added glob matching.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Should we go back and fix the release notes?
Sure, I can update as part of the 4.2.0 release notes
